prompt: Based on the clear lifestyle photos of two adults uploaded by the user, generate a high-budget wedding portrait in 'Gatsby Style / 1920s Art Deco Style'. Strictly preserve the real identity characteristics of the two people, including face shape, facial proportions, eyes, nose, lips, skin tone, sense of age, hairline, hairstyle foundation, and overall temperament. The generated result must clearly look like the same real couple/newlyweds and must not become strangers, overly Westernized, like internet celebrities, or have obvious AI fake faces. Use the uploaded photos only as identity references; do not retain the background, clothing, posture, light, or scene from the original lifestyle photos. Please generate a brand-new wedding masterpiece. The overall style is not a regular luxury wedding photo but rather: 1920s Gatsby bridal editorial, Art Deco luxury vintage wedding masterpiece, cinematic set, magazine-level finish, indoor scene with Gothic arched windows, old Hollywood glamour, mysterious, noble, dramatic, exquisite, and with a sense of ultra-high budget. The overall look should be like a high-concept project from a top wedding studio rather than a common photo studio style. Please reconstruct the scene style around the reference: tall European/Gothic arches and arched windows, strong window light, classical wooden tables and chairs, vintage candlesticks, books, light gauze, local smoke, crystal chandeliers, a few antique decorations, and dark wood floors. The space should have a sense of depth and stage presence but not turn into an overly complex palace or modern mansion. The scene is exquisite, restrained, and unified. Bridal styling is key: please explicitly create a 1920s Gatsby bride look. The wedding dress should be a high-end sequined slim-fit gown in champagne gold, silver-gold, or pearl champagne, with dense beadwork, beaded texture, a slender mermaid or straight silhouette, paired with dramatic gauze curtains, light gauze trails, exaggerated organza sleeves, or layered sleeves. Jewelry includes pearl necklaces, dangling earrings, forehead ornaments/vintage headpieces/feather headpieces/jeweled headbands, etc., overall exquisite and expensive. If the user originally has long hair, please do not directly keep the modern loose long hair effect. Please create a vintage look closer to the 1920s while maintaining the person's identity: faux bob, pinned bob / tucked long hair, finger waves, sculpted side-parted waves, low-set or curled updos, visually compressing the length into a 1920s short hair silhouette, and using vintage forehead ornaments, feathers, and jeweled hair accessories to enhance the Gatsby feel. If the user has short hair, it should also be made into a clearer 1920s finger wave/bob texture. Do not show modern daily long curly hair or common long hair. Groom styling: black, deep charcoal black, or midnight blue high-end suit/tuxedo, white shirt, black bow tie or formal bow tie, restrained and high-end brooch details. Overall tall, steady, and noble, with old money and old Hollywood leading man temperament. Light and shadow are the core of the entire set; please strictly refer to the example images: the main light source must be strong backlighting or side-backlighting from arched windows, forming visible light beams, rim light, and dramatic light-dark contrast; auxiliary light comes from candlelight and ambient warm light, serving only as local supplements; there can be a small amount of haze/smoke to make the light more layered. The overall lighting should be cinematic high-contrast lighting; avoid uniform flat light, ordinary mansion soft light, or strange washed-out highlights. Some shots allow for slight, controllable lens flare, but it must be reasonable, restrained, and from real strong light, not random glare. Please enhance the realistic photography texture: real skin texture, appropriately softened but not plastic; real weight of the gown, gauze texture, jewelry reflection, and candlelight details; avoid a 3D rendering feel, excessive sharpening, fake CG feel, or game modeling feel. The overall result should look like wedding photos taken in a real studio with physical sets. Please pay special attention to the natural and realistic proportions of the characters' faces and bodies. Both people must maintain real adult proportions, and the head size, shoulder width, neck length, arm length, palm size, physical contact, and perspective relationships must be naturally coordinated. Avoid issues like oversized faces, small heads, long necks, distorted bodies, incorrect fingers, limb fusion, a sense of stitching, or clipping. Please especially strengthen the sense of interaction and relationship between the two people; don't just stand together. There must be a clear emotional flow between the two: attraction, attachment, guardianship, tacit understanding, dramatic romance, and a bit of vow and destiny. This can be expressed through hugging from behind, facing each other with pauses, interaction by the table, approaching under the gauze, staggered looking, lightly supporting the waist or back, hand-over-hand, etc. Don't have them standing side-by-side looking at the camera in every shot. Please avoid repetition throughout the set: include full-body, half-body, close-up, and extreme close-up shots; include both solo and couple shots; include front, semi-profile, profile, and staggered angles; include quiet gazing, close-range pauses, interactions, and story scenes; don't let every shot look like a variation of a template. Negative constraints: no common mansion wedding style, no modern influencer wedding photos, no uniform flat lighting, no modern loose long curly hair, no common princess dresses, no cheap photo studio feel, no plastic skin, no characters looking like AI-synthesized stitching, no strange glare, no messy background. 8 Shot Prompts: 01 | Gauze Opening, Full Body Main Visual: Shoot a full-body main visual wedding masterpiece of the bride. The bride stands between tall arches and arched windows, with strong window light behind her, forming a sacred and dramatic backlight. A large amount of transparent light gauze sweeps by from the foreground and sides, like being gently lifted by the wind, wrapping the bride in semi-transparent light and shadow layers. The bride wears a champagne gold densely sequined high-end slim-fit wedding dress with a slender silhouette, the trail and gauze falling naturally, one hand raised to touch the gauze, the other naturally down. Emphasis: The bride must have a clear 1920s Gatsby bride styling. Even if the user has long hair, create a vintage short hair silhouette through faux bob/finger waves/pinned bob, paired with jeweled forehead ornaments, dangling earrings, and pearl necklaces. Emotion: Noble, calm, confident, like a leading lady stepping out of an old-era ball. Eyes looking lightly at or slightly past the camera, no common sweet smile. Lighting: Strong backlighting, light smoke, gauze transparency, clear light and dark layers, very strong cinematic feel. 02 | Groom Hugging from Behind, Bride in Front, Main Relationship Image: Shoot a double half-body to 3/4 body wedding masterpiece. The bride stands in front, and the groom embraces her from behind, hands naturally resting near her waist or arms, the bride's hands lightly placed on her chest or covering the groom's hands. The bride looks at the camera with a calm, expensive, and determined gaze, as the center of the story; the groom looks down at the bride with a gentle, focused, and strong sense of guardianship. Emphasis: There must be interactive tension between the two, not just standing together. The groom's presence should be like protecting and surrounding her, while the bride's state is one of being cherished and gazed upon. Physical contact must be natural, and proportions must be real. Styling and Lighting: The bride still has clear Gatsby headwear and a vintage hairstyle, with clear sequin details on the dress; the groom is in a black high-end tuxedo. Warm window backlight comes from behind, a small reasonable lens flare can be on the right, with candlelight and ambient warm light as local supplements. The overall look is more like a movie still than a common studio photo. 03 | Side View Facing Each Other, Paused at the Most Tense Second: Shoot a wedding photo of the couple facing each other from a side view. The bride and groom stand facing each other, very close, staying in the moment just before getting closer, but not actually kissing. The bride's profile is exquisite, with clear shoulder and back lines; the groom looks down at her with a calm and focused gaze. There is a strong but restrained attraction between the two, like a second before a vow. Emphasis: No smiling, no standing side-by-side. The focus is on the tension in the quiet gaze. There must be communication in their eyes, not empty or stiff. Lighting: The background is darker, with cold rim backlighting or blue-gray smoke behind the arch, outlining the characters; foreground tables, chairs, or candlesticks can slightly enter the frame. The overall style is more cinematic, low-saturation, and high-contrast. 04 | Bride Sitting at the Table, Solo Main Image: Shoot a solo sitting masterpiece of the bride. The bride sits by a vintage dark long table, with a tall arched window behind her, and candlesticks, thick books, glassware, a small amount of jewelry accessories, and light gauze on the table. The bride wears a champagne gold sequined slim-fit wedding dress, with exaggerated organza sleeves stacked around her arms and a slender skirt line. She sits slightly sideways, one hand naturally supporting the table, the other lightly on her knee or holding a vintage ornament, head slightly raised, eyes calm, noble, and slightly commanding. Emphasis: The bride still needs a 1920s vintage hairstyle, no modern long hair. The whole image should look like a hero shot in a high-budget wedding magazine. Lighting: Arched window backlight + warm candlelight supplement + local smoke. The image should have dark areas, no flat lighting. Table scenes and dress textures must be very exquisite. 05 | Bride in Foreground, Groom Watching in Background, Staggered Narrative: Shoot a double-person staggered composition wedding masterpiece. The bride sits or leans by the table as the main visual focus. She lowers her head slightly or looks into the distance, with a quiet, thoughtful emotion, as if entering an emotional pause before the wedding. The groom stands near the arched window in the background, slightly blurred or weakened, but still clearly identifiable, as if quietly watching over her. The two do not look at each other directly, but there is a clear spatial relationship and emotional connection. Emphasis: Don't make it a common double photo. This image should have a sense of plot, the bride is the visual center, and the groom exists as a silent and deep presence. Foreground and background layers must be clear. Lighting: Main light falls on the bride, with window light behind outlining the groom. Candlesticks, books, gauze, and table details should be rich but not cluttered. Overall quiet, introverted, and cinematic. 06 | Close-up of Bride's Jewelry: Shoot a cinematic close-up of the bride. The lens focuses on the bride's semi-profile, neck, vintage forehead ornament, earrings, and exquisite vintage accessories in her hand, such as metal decorations, jewelry masks, or classical small items. The bride turns her head slightly, in a semi-profile or looking-back angle, with a quiet and deep gaze, complete lip makeup, and a mature, noble look. Candles in the background are blurred into soft warm light spots. Emphasis: This shot must strongly emphasize Gatsby-style details. The headwear, finger waves/faux bob, pearl necklace, and earrings must be clear and believable. The face should not have a heavy AI feel, and the skin should not be plastic. It should look like a high-budget close-up shot for real, not a CG detail image. Lighting: Warm candlelight forms local highlights from the side and back, with soft but directional light on the face, shallow depth of field, overall very cinematic. 07 | Interaction at the Table, Sense of Ritual on the Eve of the Wedding: Shoot a double-person scene interaction wedding photo. The bride stands or leans on one side of a vintage long table, looking down to organize papers, quill pens, jewelry, or small wedding items on the table; the groom stands on the opposite side or the side of the table, gently participating in this interaction, such as handing her a small item, helping her light a candle, or quietly watching her. The two are in the same scene with very natural interaction, not a standard standing position photo. Emphasis: This image should have the atmosphere of the 'night before the wedding ceremony.' The interaction between the two must be natural, like a captured fragment from a movie. The bride is still the visual focus, and the groom exists as companionship and guardianship. Lighting: Window light is dominant, candlelight is local atmospheric light, and the table and prop details are clear. Overall, there is a sense of classical ritual and narrative. 08 | Intimate Closing Image Under the Veil: Shoot a double-person closing wedding masterpiece. The bride and groom stand by the table or arched window, with a long veil or light gauze hanging from above their heads, wrapping them in the same semi-transparent space. The groom's hand lightly supports the bride's waist or back, and the bride's hand rests on the groom's chest. Their foreheads are close, or the groom gently leans close to the bride's forehead, staying in the moment with the most emotional tension, not actually kissing. The bride looks at the groom or lowers her eyes, and the groom looks at her lightly or closes his eyes slightly; the overall feel is extremely intimate but restrained. Emphasis: This is not a 'kissing photo' but a 'pause before the vows.' There must be a strong interaction and sense of relationship between the two. The gauze in the foreground, candlelight, and table details must all serve this emotion. Lighting: Overall dark, window backlight and candlelight together shape the scene, the gauze shines semi-transparently in the light, and the image is sacred, noble, and dramatic, like the closing shot of the entire set.
